rationale: Builds directly on the current best Late Projection EMA Finalizer instead of restarting. Recent projection-EMA row/clip variants have flattened, so this compounds the winning late EMA path with a different lever from an earlier close miss: keep the projection payload int8 and the training loop unchanged, but store per-row scales and offsets for attn/MLP projection matrices in fp32. That spends a small amount of the remaining size budget on more faithful final dequantization exactly where the current best already found the most quantization-sensitive weights, while preserving the late projection EMA endgame and adding no hot-loop cost.
title: Projection EMA With FP32 Metadata
